title: Illinois: no general deletion right, but strong biometric protection
summary: Illinois has not enacted a comprehensive consumer-privacy law with a general right to delete. But its Biometric Information Privacy Act (BIPA) strongly regulates how businesses collect and keep biometric identifiers such as faceprints and fingerprints, including a private right to sue. For general removal, use the data broker's own opt-out (below).
disclaimer: General information, not legal advice, and not yet independently reviewed. BIPA covers biometric data specifically; verify scope before relying on it.
